posts.content: I'm being accused of overhyping the [site everyone heard too much about today already].
posts.highlights: Karpathy responds to criticism of overhyping a popular site.

posts.content: Power to the people: How LLMs flip the script on technology diffusion. So it strikes me as quite unique and remarkable that LLMs display a dramatic reversal of this pattern - they generate disproportionate benefit for regular people, while their impact is a lot more...
posts.highlights: Karpathy argues that LLMs benefit regular people more than experts, reversing typical tech diffusion.
posts.worthReading: Offers a fresh perspective on LLM societal impact.

posts.content: There's a new kind of coding I call "vibe coding", where you fully give in to the vibes, embrace exponentials, and forget that the code even exists. Also I just talk to Composer with SuperWhisper.
posts.highlights: Karpathy coins 'vibe coding' as an AI-assisted coding style where developers rely on AI and ignore code details.
posts.worthReading: Introduces a popular concept shaping AI-assisted development.

posts.content: It's interesting how "better at code" has become the defining goal of almost every AI lab over the
posts.highlights: Simon notes that AI labs are increasingly focused on improving code generation capabilities.

posts.content: TL;DR: More and more agents need a workspace: a computer where they can run code, install packages, and access files. Sandboxes provide this
posts.highlights: Agents require a sandboxed workspace to execute code, install packages, and access files.
posts.worthReading: Highlights a key infrastructure need for agent deployment.

posts.content: When building agents, you need to iterate on production data much more than when building traditional software. You need to iterate on how
posts.highlights: Agent development requires more iteration on production data than traditional software.

posts.content: Traditional Application Performance Monitoring (APM) tools focus on metrics like latency, traffic, errors, and saturation. They track HTTP

posts.content: It was always the case that agency was self-compounding, but AI is magnifying the effect. Low-agency AI users further lose agency, high-agency AI users further gain agency.
posts.highlights: Agency compounds with AI use: low-agency users lose more agency, high-agency users gain more.
posts.worthReading: Highlights a critical dynamic of AI adoption that affects user autonomy.

posts.content: Current AI is a librarian of existing knowledge. Science requires an explorer of the unknown.

posts.worthReading: Articulates a fundamental limitation of current AI systems.

posts.content: We're facing an LLM bubble, not a broader AI bubble. The industry is obsessed with building one massive model when we should be focusing on practical applications.
